preview_text:
The model favors Canada at 41% to win, with Switzerland trailing at 28%. The underdog's path is narrower but far from foreclosed. A draw ranks second in likelihood at 31%, suggesting this match carries genuine competitive tension.

The win–loss spread reflects a moderately open contest. Canada holds the statistical edge, yet the draw probability remains substantial—nearly matching Switzerland's outright win chance. This distribution indicates the teams are closer in the model's assessment than a simple head-to-head win probability might suggest, with multiple plausible outcomes.

The market and Polymarket consensus diverge notably from the model. Both sportsbooks and crypto traders assign Switzerland roughly 44% to win—a 16 percentage point gap above the model's 28%. Conversely, both sources credit Canada with only 27%, some 13 points below the model. Draw probabilities align closely across all three sources. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct; the divergence warrants attention from users.
